items.label: Development Complete — Schemas
messages: Now let me explore the existing schemas structure to understand the patterns.
Let me read the actual schema files directly to understand the existing patterns.
I now have full context on the existing patterns. Let me implement the webhook schemas.
Now update the barrel export:
Now add comprehensive tests for the webhook schemas:
Now append the webhook schema tests at the end of the test file:
Now let me build and test:
Build succeeded. Now run tests:
All 53 tests pass (including 23 new webhook schema tests). Now let me run the post-change review and commit:
Review shows: no breaking changes, moderate risk score (purely from novelty of new files), minimal structural impact (1.6% of graph). All clean. Now commit and complete:

All done. Here's a summary of what was completed:  ## Schema Development Complete ✅  ### Files Created - **`packages/schemas/src/webhooks.ts`** — Three Zod schemas as specified:   - `WebhookSchema` — Full webhook record (`id`, `workspaceId`, `url` with `.url()` validation, `createdAt` with `.datetime()` validation)   - `CreateWebhookRequestSchema` — POST request body (`url` with `.url()`, `workspaceId`)   - `WebhookListResponseSchema` — GET response envelope (`webhooks: z.array(WebhookSchema)`)   - All three types exported via `z.infer<>`  ### Files Modified - **`packages/schemas/src/index.ts`** — Added barrel re-exports for all 3 schemas + 3 types following existing pattern - **`packages/schemas/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts`** — Added 23 new round-trip tests covering:   - Valid parsing, missing fields, invalid URLs, non-ISO dates, extra field stripping, empty objects, empty arrays, multiple items, invalid items in arrays  ### Validation - ✅ `npm run build` — TypeScript compiles clean - ✅ `npx jest --verbose` — All 53 tests pass (30 existing + 23 new) - ✅ No breaking changes (additive only) - ✅ Roam review: minimal structural impact (1.6% of graph), zero breaking changes

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh backend "feat(schemas): add webhook dispatcher Zod schemas (WebhookSchema, CreateWebhookRequestSchema, WebhookListResponseSchem
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:complete webhook-dispatcher schema-dev 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark Development Complete — Schemas" 2>&1

items.label: Infrastructure Written — Terraform
messages: Now let me check the existing hooks to understand their patterns:

I have a clear picture of the existing infrastructure. Now let me implement all the required changes.  **Intent: Creating Cosmos DB Terraform config, updating APIM OpenAPI spec, outputs, and Function App settings.**
Now update main.tf to add the `COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT` app setting:
Now add the Cosmos DB outputs:
Now update the OpenAPI spec to add webhook endpoints:
Now update the validate-infra.sh hook with a Cosmos DB reachability check:
Now let me run Terraform validation:
Terraform validate passes. Now let me run the full plan:
Terraform plan succeeds: **4 to add, 4 to change, 0 to destroy**. The new Cosmos DB resources and outputs are planned correctly.  Let me verify the resources being added:

All 4 new resources are being created as expected: - `azurerm_cosmosdb_account.main`  - `azurerm_cosmosdb_sql_database.main` - `azurerm_cosmosdb_sql_container.webhooks` - `azurerm_cosmosdb_sql_role_assignment.func_data_contributor`  And 4 updates: - `azurerm_api_management_api.sample` — updated OpenAPI spec with webhook paths - `azurerm_function_app_flex_consumption.main` — new COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT app setting - Two drift-related in-place updates (key vault secret, SWA)  Now let me commit and complete:
Now let me leave a doc-note and mark complete:

All infrastructure work is complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:  ## Infrastructure Changes Summary  ### New File: `infra/cosmos.tf` - **`azurerm_cosmosdb_account.main`** — Serverless Cosmos DB SQL API account (`cosmos-sample-app-{suffix}`) with Session consistency - **`azurerm_cosmosdb_sql_database.main`** — Database `sample-app-db` - **`azurerm_cosmosdb_sql_container.webhooks`** — Container `Webhooks` with partition key `/workspaceId` - **`azurerm_cosmosdb_sql_role_assignment.func_data_contributor`** — Function App managed identity → Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor (zero-key auth via `DefaultAzureCredential`)  ### Modified: `infra/main.tf` - Added `COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT` app setting to `azurerm_function_app_flex_consumption.main`, referencing `azurerm_cosmosdb_account.main.endpoint`  ### Modified: `infra/outputs.tf` - Added `cosmosdb_endpoint` output (account endpoint URL) - Added `cosmosdb_account_name` output  ### Modified: `infra/api-specs/api-sample.openapi.yaml` - Added `GET /webhooks` (`listWebhooks`) with optional `workspaceId` query param - Added `POST /webhooks` (`createWebhook`) with `url` + `workspaceId` request body - Added `Webhook` component schema with `id`, `workspaceId`, `url`, `createdAt`  ### Modified: `.apm/hooks/validate-infra.sh` - Added Cosmos DB reachability check reading `COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT` env var  ### Validation - ✅ `terraform validate` — passed - ✅ `terraform plan` — **4 to add, 4 to change, 0 to destroy**

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh infra "feat(infra): provision Cosmos DB serverless account, database, Webhooks container with RBAC; add COSMOSDB_ENDPOINT to Fu
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:doc-note webhook-dispatcher infra-architect "## Terraform Resources Created
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:complete webhook-dispatcher infra-architect
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh infra "chore(pipeline): mark infra-architect complete for webhook-dispatcher"

items.label: Draft PR Created
messages: The `gh pr list` output was empty (no existing PR). Now let me get the repo info and create the draft PR.
Good. Repo is `RomanKaliupinMelonusa/DAGent-t` and we're on branch `feature/webhook-dispatcher`. Now let me create the PR body file and the draft PR.
Draft PR #35 created. Now let me clean up and update the pipeline state.

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && gh pr list --head "feature/webhook-dispatcher" --json number -q '.[0].number' 2>/dev/null || echo ""
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:complete webhook-dispatcher create-draft-pr 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:set-note webhook-dispatcher "Draft PR #35 created — awaiting Terraform plan" 2>&1
